channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The 'Saturday Night Live' 50th anniversary celebration is finally here. Here's how to watch
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fifty seasons of “Saturday Night Live” sketches, songs and special guests will be celebrated Sunday as the variety show celebrates its landmark anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department's independence is threatened as Trump's team asserts power over cases and staff
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pam Bondi had insisted at her Senate confirmation hearing that as attorney general, her Justice Department would not “play politics.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 3 dead in fiery Wyoming tunnel crash
item.description: Another person has been confirmed dead as the investigation continues into a major, multi-vehicle crash in the westbound tunnel of I-80 in Green River, Wyoming, on Friday, according to the Wyoming Dept. of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration wants the Supreme Court to let the firing of whistleblower agency head proceed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration wants the Supreme Court to permit the firing of the head of the federal agency dedicated to protecting whistleblowers, according to documents obtained Sunday that would mark the first appeal to the justices since President Donald Trump took office.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly Austria stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Does your kid have 'hurried child syndrome'? How to tell and what to do
item.description: The syndrome doesn't come from you rushing your kid to get dressed, but a different set of expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some in Hurricane Helene-ravaged North Carolina embrace Trump's push to abolish FEMA
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — Emily Russell remembers feeling hopeful after she managed to get an appointment with the Federal Emergency Management Agency not long after Hurricane Helene ripped though her home in Swannanoa, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: GREEN RIVER, Wyo. (AP) — Investigators in Wyoming began sifting through the wreckage inside a highway tunnel Saturday after smoke, hazardous chemicals and structural concerns prevented them from determining how many people may have died in the fiery pileup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off thousands of probationary workers in the middle of tax season, according to two sources familiar with the agency's plans, and cuts could happen as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NAACP lists companies that dump DEI in its tactical spending guide for Black Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The NAACP wants Black Americans to steer their buying power toward companies that haven’t pulled back from diversity, equity and inclusion programs under conservative pressure, and the nation’s oldest civil rights organization is listing which brands have stood by — or reversed — p
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: Officials with the Flagler County Sheriff's Office are expected to remain active in the area for several days as recovery operations continue.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Back-to-back earthquakes jolt Los Angeles area within hours
item.description: An earthquake with a preliminary magnitude of 3.5 rattled the L.A. metropolitan area early Saturday just hours after a 3.8-magnitude quake shook the Malibu area shortly before midnight Friday.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Suzanne Simpson murder case: Texas defense for missing realtor's husband looking for holes in evidence trove
item.description: Suzanne Simpson vanished over four months ago, and her husband, charged with her murder, made an appearance in court, where attorneys discussed evidence in the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UFO expert says Trump's declassification could expose possible 'cover-up' spanning decades
item.description: An expert on UFOs breaks down the importance of President Trump declassifying government files he believes holds "the smoking gun" and answers to federal secrets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss N.Y.C. Mayor Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KTLA 5

item.title: Dozens of couples get hitched at snowy Colorado ski resort on Valentine's Day
item.description: LOVELAND SKI AREA, Colo. (AP) — There is nothing like dozens of couples saying “I do” simultaneously to warm the crisp, frigid air that grips the Colorado Rockies in the heart of winter.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Left-hander Cole Ragans and Royals agree to $13.25 million, 3-year contract
item.description: All-Star left-hander Cole Ragans and the Kansas City Royals agreed Friday to a $13.25 million, three-year contract.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Andrew Lester pleads guilty in shooting of unarmed Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl
item.description: Andrew Lester pleaded guilty to second-degree assault after shooting 16-year-old Ralph Yarl outside Kansas City, Missouri home in April 2023
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KRON4

item.title: DOGE gets mixed reviews from conservatives who have long wanted major budget cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Conservatives who have spent decades promoting deep cuts to government are giving mixed reviews to the Department of Government Efficiency, the Elon Musk-led project to slash the federal workforce and reduce or end disfavored programs.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, KRON4

item.title: Would-be Indiana school shooter had collage of mass murderers, court docs reveal
item.description: The student said in January that the shooting would be “Parkland part two” and that they had been planning it for a year.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Thursday that her government wouldn't rule out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 100 vehicles involved in Oregon highway crash amid 'whiteout' conditions
item.description: More than 100 cars, trucks and semi-trucks were involved in a crash along Oregon's Interstate 84 early Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, WFLA, NBC

item.title: CBP officer pleads not guilty to migrant smuggling, drug conspiracy
item.description: A U.S. Customs and Border Protection officer charged with multiple counts of violating immigration and drug laws has pleaded not guilty to the charges, court documents show.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Igloo recalling 1M+ coolers after fingertip amputations
item.description: Igloo has received 12 reports of fingertip injuries, including fingertip amputations, bone fractures and slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Judge blocks Trump’s gender-affirming care executive orders nationwide
item.description: BALTIMORE — A federal judge Thursday blocked parts of two executive orders issued by President Trump that collectively seek to restrict gender-affirming care. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Modi's visit with Trump could boost US-India relations amid tariff woes
item.description: Modi is a nationalist and has talked up his warm relationship with Trump during his first term while cheering his winning back the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma governor says he'll stop plan to collect kids' immigration status
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt said he will stop a plan pushed by the state’s top education official to collect the immigration status of children when they register for public school.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US aircraft carrier collides with merchant vessel in Mediterranean Sea
item.description: The collision occurred between the Nimitz-class aircraft carrier USS Harry S. Truman (CVN 75) and the merchant vessel Besiktas-M, according to U.S. Navy spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9
